_comment: Held-out defect-finding benchmark for the panel-vs-baseline experiment. Each item shows real pre-fix code; the task is to name the most serious defect. `key` is a RUBRIC (answers are prose), not an exact string. SEED ONLY: these are drawn from this repo's own changelog, where the fix is the answer key. Extend to n>=30 with defects from other repos with a known key before the real run (see PREREGISTRATION.md).

grader_calibration.key: answer must identify that the loop condition uses <= with array length, reading one element past the end
grader_calibration.answer: The loop runs from i=0 while i<=len, but valid indices are 0..len-1, so at i==len it reads one element past the end of the array — an off-by-one out-of-bounds read.

grader_calibration.key: answer must identify that the mutex is locked but never unlocked on the early-return path, causing a deadlock
grader_calibration.answer: On the `if err != nil` branch the function returns without calling Unlock(), so the mutex stays held and the next caller deadlocks.

grader_calibration.key: answer must identify that the loop condition uses <= with array length, reading one element past the end
grader_calibration.answer: The variable names are unclear and the function is too long; it should be split for readability.

grader_calibration.key: answer must identify that the mutex is locked but never unlocked on the early-return path, causing a deadlock
grader_calibration.answer: The error is not wrapped with context, so debugging the failure will be harder.

questions.key: answer must identify that the winner is the sanitized key (spaces/punctuation stripped, e.g. UseJWTwithsessions) while calculate_final_score compares each consultant's ORIGINAL approach string against it, so they never match and even a unanimous multiword panel is scored as all-dissent (~2/10 instead of 10/10)

questions.prompt:
A cost function was changed to recurse so it would catch debate rounds written under round_N/ subdirectories. Name the most serious defect and why.

calculate_session_cost() {
  local dir="$1" total=0
  while IFS= read -r f; do
    model=$(jq -r '.model // "default"' "$f")
    tokens=$(jq -r '.metadata.tokens_used // 1000' "$f")
    total=$(echo "$total + $(cost_for "$model" "$tokens")" | bc)
  done < <(find "$dir" -type f -name '*.json')
  echo "$total"
}

# Context: the run directory also contains a peer_review/ subtree with
# anonymized copies of each response (which keep the original .metadata.tokens_used)
# and bookkeeping files like mapping.json (which have no .metadata at all).
questions.key: answer must identify that the recursive find also sweeps in the peer_review/ subtree: anonymized copies re-bill the original tokens (double counting) and bookkeeping files with no metadata take the // 1000 fallback, inflating the reported cost; the recursion needed to be scoped to the responses plus round_N/ only

answer must identify that an explicit empty-string argument is indistinguishable from no argument (both make text empty), so estimate_tokens "" falls into cat and blocks reading stdin forever whenever stdin is an open pipe rather than closed/EOF; it should decide by argument count ($#), not by emptiness

questions.prompt:
This function runs under `set -euo pipefail`. It is meant to warn only in CLI mode and otherwise do nothing. Name the most serious defect and why.

warn_if_cli() {
  local agent="$1"
  local var="${agent}_EFFORT"
  [[ -n "${!var:-}" ]] || return 0
  is_api_mode "$agent" && return 0
  log_warn "$var is ignored in CLI mode"
}
questions.key: answer must identify that `is_api_mode "$agent" && return 0` is an &&-list: when is_api_mode returns non-zero (CLI mode) the whole list evaluates to non-zero, and because it is the last command before log_warn under set -e, a caller can be aborted / the function returns non-zero unexpectedly; it must use an explicit if-block, not an &&-list, as the guard

answer must identify that the documented ${AGENT}_FORMAT override variables are never read — the function hardcodes the mapping by agent name — so setting QWEN3_FORMAT=openai silently does nothing, and there is no way to point a consultant at an endpoint using a different wire protocol

answer must identify that tokens_used is hardcoded to 0 for every response, so real consultants contribute $0 to the cost; the reported figure comes only from other files the cost glob picks up (which take a fallback token count), so the number reflects pipeline overhead, not actual spend

answer must identify that the test asserts on log_warn output but does not pin LOG_LEVEL, so it is non-hermetic: for anyone whose environment sets LOG_LEVEL=ERROR the warning is suppressed and the assertion fails deterministically for that environment while passing everywhere else; the suite must export LOG_LEVEL=INFO before the assertion

answer must identify that select_consultants filters only on affinity and ignores the ENABLE_* flags and self-exclusion, so with smart routing on, a consultant the user disabled (or the invoking agent itself) can still be selected, queried, and billed; eligibility must be applied here where the output is consumed
